entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 9 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-09 03:47AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • AFL 2022 round 17 LIVE updates: Magpies aim for seven in a row against battling North
  • ‘He fears it’: The Kyrgios weapon Djokovic’s camp could never conquer
  • Horn torn as “battle of the brothers” reignites Tszyu rivalry
  • Violent West Leederville fight left multiple teens with serious injuries
  • Nine-year-old in pyjamas shot by man allegedly ‘laying in wait’
  • Kim Kardashian's daughter North West has a message for the paparazzi.
  • What an opera singer puts on his plate each day
  • Hawthorn mourn death of Norm Smith medallist Paul Dear
  • ‘Sick obsession with tearing me down’: Kyrgios unhappy with former Australian stars
  • Labuschagne and Smith salute on a strong opening day for Australia
  • I am 58 with little super. Should I panic?
  • Woman slammed for wearing 'off-white' gold dress to wedding
  • Best of cartoons, July 9, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • Elon Musk terminates multibillion-dollar Twitter deal
  • Natalie Portman under fire for exaggerating LGBTQ elements in 'Thor'
  • Munster and Taulagi out of Origin after positive COVID-19 tests
  • $150 million worth of drugs allegedly concealed in machinery: police
  • Travel chaos across airports
  • Amber Heard asks court to declare Johnny Depp case a mistrial
  • Clive Palmer’s $100 million senator reveals his political agenda
  • Retail, hospitality heads want pandemic payments back as cases surge
  • The latest illustrations from artist Michael Leunig

  • NSW attorney-general seeks advice as murder trial ends without verdict after accused died
  • Timor-Leste swindle must be investigated
  • Leisl Smith murder trial ends without a verdict after accused killer takes his own life
  • Loving two people a ‘national sport’ in France, Chris Dawson’s barrister says
  • A look back at a forgotten Sydney
  • World shocked by the assassination of former Japanese PM
  • Shinzo Abe, Japan’s longest-serving PM, failed to achieve a key long-held dream
  • Sexual harassment scandals helped bring down Johnson. He won’t be the last
  • Plight of basketball star presents a Putin problem for Biden
  • Biden signs executive orders to safeguard abortion as frustration mounts
  • Wong meets Wang as ministers attempt to stabilise China-Australia relations

  • Former Japanese prime minister Shinzo Abe has died after being shot during a speech in Nara.

  • Far from former leader, Abe was synonymous with Japan’s centre of power
  • The Boris paradox
  • Former Japanese PM Shinzo Abe assassinated
  • Former Japanese prime minister shot while giving speech
  • Boris Johnson resigns as UK prime minister
  • Shinzo Abe shooting a wake up call to the developed world
  • Russia must be held to account, says Wong as invasion slammed at G20
  • ‘Unsustainable’: Tories push to remove Johnson before successor is picked
  • Former Japanese prime minister Shinzo Abe shot while giving speech
  • Ukraine is heading for tragedy, Putin warns West
  • Boris Johnson has quit, so who’s in for the Tory leadership?
  • Shadow minister for foreign affairs issues stark warning about assassination attempts
  • Djokovic books date with Kyrgios in final
  • Police investigate links to gangs after young girl shot in Sydney
  • Elon Musk backflips on $44 billion Twitter deal
  • Breaking the wave: What will it take for us to get ahead of COVID-19?
  • A different message for our third COVID winter
  • Now is the hour for Albanese, small-target man, to grow in stature
  • ‘I’m still gagged’: Jaguar Jonze on the cost of speaking out
  • Walking in Melbourne, an e-scooter left Julia with serious injuries and a massive hospital bill
  • As China hawks fly, we need heretics
  • The church with no faith in its women
  • It’s time for men-only clubs to open their doors to the other 50 per cent
  • ‘Dangerous to the public’: Celebrity cosmetic surgeon suspended amid probe
  • Crypto vertigo: Dizzying tale of an Aussie ‘scammer’ who says he was set up
  • Retirement and mixed-use developments to focus on sustainable living
  • Hengyi looks to offload Abbotsford office block
  • ‘I’m done with crypto’: Voyager bankruptcy rocks true believers
  • Putin’s desperation on show as he slowly loses the financial battle
  • ‘Underperforming and expensive’: Magellan facing critical test after another $5b in outflows
  • From jailhouse blues to The Voice: The program helping prisoners make music
  • Western Sydney hip-hop has won over the world. Will it get its moment at home?
  • Aussie models pack Paris couture catwalks in Kidman’s slipstream
  • Back on track, Packer ‘building something beautiful’ with Robert De Niro
  • Swag on the Beat's TikTok comedy
  • Aspiring comedians like Aurelia St Clair are trying to make it on TikTok
  • What to read: A powerful page-turner and bad relations in Britain
  • When mother knew best ... or did she?
  • Here are the year’s best books for younger readers
  • Universal Music boss’ sudden exit sparks dramatic leadership change
  • Hyped for decades, truly immersive VR is still stuck in the imagination
  • National weather forecast for Friday July 09
  • Charting Sydney’s recent rainfall, river levels and dam capacity
  • Floodwaters recede as thousands return home to assess damage
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • 38 investigated for multiple offences after multi-agency raids in Geylang area

entry.title: NY Times: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 9 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-09 03:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: should Hong Kong be concerned about global rise in BA.4 and BA.5 cases? Here’s what we know about more transmissive Omicron subvariants
  • Hong Kong-mainland China scientific exchanges, research in limbo as pandemic travel restrictions drag on
  • UN Human Rights Committee grills Hong Kong officials on police handling of 2019 protests, appointment of national security law judges
  • Hong Kong police issue warning over impersonating officers after Jimmy Lai’s London-based lawyers sent threatening email from ‘National Security Wing’
  • Hong Kong court approves extradition of resident Ramanjit Singh to India for alleged role in notorious jailbreak
  • Booklets containing speech Chinese President Xi Jinping delivered in Hong Kong go on sale at bookstores
  • Arrest warrant issued for former Hong Kong opposition party vice-chairman after no-show over allegations of false claims about face masks
  • Video: Hong Kong after the handover: the highs and lows of 25 years under ‘one country, two systems’
  • Video: A look back at Hong Kong 25 years since the handover
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong proposes closed-loop border reopening with mainland China, under which residents quarantine locally before crossing over; 2,945 cases logged
  • Police arrest 7 over knife fight at construction site of Hong Kong airport’s third runway, hours after it first opened for take-off and landing drills
  • Firefighters battle blaze at Hong Kong mansion owned by former screen goddess Brigitte Lin and billionaire husband
  • College representatives from Chinese University of Hong Kong in talks for new student union 9 months after disbandment of 50-year-old body
  • On top of censorship, Chinese writers now have to contend with attacks from patriotic trolls, and that’s worrying
  • US-China trade war: Competing interests tug at Joe Biden as he mulls cutting tariffs on Chinese goods
  • Beijing grapples with Shinzo Abe’s mixed legacy on China-Japan relations after former prime minister is shot dead
  • South Korean firms fret over China supply-chain disruptions - with ‘chaos’ to continue
  • Xi Jinping allies lay out Chinese president’s global risk outlook in countdown to Communist Party congress
  • Chinese embassy slams India’s tech firm probes after Vivo, Xiaomi and Huawei investigations, fines
  • Nationalists attack Chinese journalist over emotional Shinzo Abe report
  • China’s former police vice-minister Sun Lijun pleads guilty to bribery, stocks fraud
  • Sound can numb pain in mice, paving way for alternative treatment, Chinese scientist says
  • ByteDance executive who co-founded Musical.ly app, later absorbed by TikTok, quits tech unicorn amid corporate restructuring
  • China’s coronavirus recovery at ‘critical point’, says Li Keqiang, singling out five regions to carry the economy
  • China-Britain relations expected to face further challenges no matter who replaces Boris Johnson as prime minister
  • China to implement from September strict new cross-border data transfer rules, complicating operations of international firms
  • China-backed AIIB picks Abu Dhabi for first overseas office, more could follow
  • Air China responds to viral video of loose wing screw on Boeing 737-NG flight
  • Shinzo Abe: a political titan who shaped Japan’s policies on China, defence, economy
  • China premier stresses data security after alleged leak affecting 1 billion Chinese residents triggers concerns
  • Beijing delivers double warning to US on Taiwan Strait tensions
  • ‘Inflation jihad’, cash handouts: Can Singapore, Malaysia and other Asian countries soften the blow of rising prices?
  • Upholding common law, tacking vested interests: Xi’s vision of Hong Kong strikes a chord
  • Lack of global consensus bodes ill for health of world’s oceans
  • How Shinzo Abe’s killer thwarted Japan’s strict gun laws
  • Shinzo Abe shooting: who’s killer Tetsuya Yamagami and what weapon did he use?
  • Coronavirus: ‘New wave’ in Malaysia driven by Omicron BA.5, health minister says
  • Russia leaves G20 Bali meeting early, as ‘frenzied criticism’ over Ukraine clouds Indonesia’s multilateralism push
  • Philippines’ Marcos tests positive for Covid-19 two days after meeting with China’s Wang Yi
  • World leaders from China to Singapore and Australia stunned by death of Japan’s Shinzo Abe
  • Philippine Nobel laureate Ressa loses appeal against cyber libel conviction
  • Shinzo Abe shooting: UK finance minister Nadhim Zahawi deletes tweet saying Japan ex-PM is dead
  • Did South Korean police use ‘excessive force’ to subdue a Vietnamese man holding a knife?
  • WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange ‘will die’ if extradited to US, Australian journalist John Pilger says
  • The Boris Johnson era is ending. What about Westminster sleaze culture?
  • Ukraine says no power, water in Russian-held Sievierodonetsk, bodies rotting in flats
  • Elon Musk says he’s terminating US$44 billion Twitter deal
  • Daughter of Angola ex-president Jose Eduardo dos Santos demands autopsy on foul play fears
  • Russia and other nuclear-armed parties must be held to account for violations of non-proliferation treaty
  • Joe Biden signs executive order on abortion protection, calls US Supreme Court ‘out of control’
  • Prince Harry wins first stage in libel suit against UK newspaper
  • Breaking up HSBC: Ping An’s push to extract value from bank’s split fails to excite shareholders as unorthodox campaign enters its third month
  • The Nothing Phone 1 is Carl Pei’s first move in trying to shake up the smartphone since leaving OnePlus. Will it work?
  • Why US talk of ‘friend-shoring’ supply chains makes no economic sense
  • China Great Wall International’s bond sees record slump as investors worry about a Huarong-like crisis
  • Fred Ma, former Hong Kong minister and FWD director, appointed chairman of insurer backed by Richard Li
  • China Resources buys into HQ of struggling Hong Kong cosmetic retailer Bonjour Holdings via joint venture for US$114.7 million
  • Luxury brand Tom Ford hires Goldman Sachs to explore potential sale that could be valued in the billions of dollars
  • Why Asia’s gradual Covid-19 recovery means it has less to fear from rising inflation
  • How Hong Kong’s longest-running life insurer will help drive city’s recovery as it marks 125th anniversary
  • What are China’s ‘sponge cities’? The urban flooding solution that’s being trialed across the country to counter an increasingly deadly problem
  • Why good dental hygiene is essential to reduce inflammation and slow the onset of chronic diseases and ‘inflammaging’
  • Joel Kim Booster’s recent forays into movie producing and Netflix specials – and why his job ‘isn’t to represent all of you’
  • Lake Como villa that once hosted Winston Churchill now a luxury hotel, and Kate Moss and Martha Stewart are fans
  • Squid Game, BTS fuel interest in Korea, but Korean studies courses at universities don’t benefit – they lack funding to attract more students, say experts
  • Fine dining or casual, French restaurants in Hong Kong are alive and well, as recent openings Auor and Terroirs by LQV show
  • Vitamin D from sunshine could help keep dementia at bay, a study shows, and fight other diseases
  • K-drama actor and Twenty-Five Twenty-One star Nam Joo-hyuk hit by more allegations of school bullying
  • Worn by celebrities like Zendaya and model Emily Ratajkowski, fashion designer Nensi Dojaka’s clothes explore interplay between sensuality and strength
  • Meet Extraordinary Attorney Woo’s Park Eun-bin, who appeared in a Taeyeon MV and alongside Lee Byung-hun and Squid Game’s Lee Jung-jae as a child star aged just six
  • Meet Ricky Martin’s adorable teen twins, Matteo and Valentino: the 50-year-old singer is raising his kids with hubby Jwan Yosef, enjoying the beach, jets and Grammys red carpets
  • Celebs who got swindled: Shark Tank star Barbara Corcoran almost lost US$400,000, Anne Hathaway got scammed out of US$2.4 million by her ex-boyfriend, and Robert De Niro got conned investing in art
  • Meet Shivon Zilis, Elon Musk’s baby mama who had twins: the tech whizz met the world’s richest man and Tesla CEO through her AI work – and fangirls over him on Twitter
  • The fabulous life of Penelope Disick: as Kourtney Kardashian and Scott Disick’s daughter turns 10, it’s clear the jet-setting, red-haired Yeezy and Skims wearing tween is a chip off the old block
  • How many children does Elon Musk have now? His 9 (and counting) kids revealed, from new twins with Shivon Zilis, and baby X Æ A-Xii, to trans daughter Vivian Wilson ... meet his brood and their mum
  • Inside Boris Johnson’s controversial lavish life as Britain’s prime minister, from his US$2 million net worth to dropping thousands on take-out, home refurbishments and that NYE trip
  • Selling Sunset’s Christine Quinn listed this US$34 million Miami penthouse through her new firm – take a look inside the ultra-luxury, 43rd floor home that you can even buy with cryptocurrency
  • How Fendi got East-West fusion right at Paris Fashion Week: Kim Jones’ autumn/winter 2022-23 haute couture collection made style stops in Kyoto, Paris and Rome – tastefully
  • 6 best new Hong Kong restaurants with spectacular sea views, from ADD+ and Mosu in West Kowloon’s M+, to Salisterra and Radical Chic, which have new chefs
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
